meta.title: beta2 Microglobulin on the envelope of urinary cytomegalovirus is not associated with host class I human leukocyte antigen {alpha} chain

abstractHtml:
Previous studies have shown that 2 microglobulin (2m) is associated with glycoproteins present on the envelope of urinary human cytomegalovirus (CMV). 2m is non-covalently associated with the α chain of human leukocyte antigen (HLA) class I antigens and therefore it was of interest to determine whether the class I α chain is also associated with the 2mCMV complex. Using a panel of monoclonal antibodies recognizing different conformational determinants on the HLA class I heterodimer or free 2m, we have shown that 2m but not the α chain of HLA class I could be immunoprecipitated from 125I-surface-labelled virions purified directly from urine. We therefore conclude that host class I HLA α chains are not associated with 2m on the envelope of urinary CMV.

† Present address: National Institute for Biological Standards and Control, Blanche Lane, South Mimms, Potters Bar EN6 3QG, U.K.
